full_prompt: Design a monolithic aluminum 7020-T6 swingarm for an electric motorcycle swingarm benchmark (collegiate petrol-category prototype racing motorcycle) using a spec liquid-cooled four-stroke 250 cc single producing 30 kW and 27 N-m at the countershaft sprocket, within the Moto3-adjacent geometry envelope prescribed in the benchmark regulations. The swingarm is a single extruded-and-machined 7020-T6 aluminum body (no welds in the primary structure; it is machined from a single forged billet or equivalent CNC envelope), with integral chain-adjuster slots and integral rear axle forks. Geometry: pivot-axis-to-rear-axle length 560 mm (centre-to-centre), pivot bore 22 mm ID x 115 mm wide across both pivot lugs, rear axle slot 22 mm diameter (adjuster range 20 mm), twin-sided box-section arms each 60 mm wide x 45 mm tall with wall thickness 5 mm, bridge 200 mm aft of pivot for lateral rigidity 40 mm wide x 35 mm tall x 4 mm wall, brake-caliper mounting ear with 2x M10 holes on 82 mm centers on the left arm, rear axle nut clearance 35 mm. Total bounding envelope 580 mm x 250 mm x 100 mm. Material 7020-T6 aluminum (EN AW-7020) with Young's modulus 72 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.33, density 2780 kg/m^3, yield 290 MPa, UTS 345 MPa; fatigue endurance limit 90 MPa at 10^7 cycles (welded joint derated, but for monolithic billet the base-metal value 130 MPa applies and a further 0.7 surface factor yields 90 MPa conservatively). Three load cases per FIM racing-motorcycle frame structural practice and benchmark chassis guidelines Section 4. Load Case 1 (longitudinal drive + bump): 4.0 kN horizontal chain-pull force forward on the rear axle simulating peak drive torque (27 N-m x 5.5 primary ratio x 14-tooth front sprocket pitch radius gives ~4.0 kN at the swingarm pivot), combined with 3.5g vertical bump-load on the rear axle centered on rider + bike mass 195 kg distributed 40% rear = 2.7 kN; pivot bore fully constrained. Load Case 2 (cornering + braking): 3.5 kN lateral force at the rear axle (cornering), combined with 1.8 kN longitudinal rearward force from 1.0g rear-wheel braking. Load Case 3 (wheelie / landing): 5.0g vertical down-load at the rear axle following a wheelie landing, 4.9 kN vertical. Pass/fail: (R1) peak von Mises under LC1, LC2, LC3 must not exceed 145 MPa (yield/2.0 per FIM chassis practice); (R2) fatigue check: under an assumed 10^7-cycle service spectrum using LC1 stress range scaled to 40% of LC1 peak, the local stress range at any notch (axle slot, pivot bore, caliper-mount ear) must remain below 90 MPa; (R3) maximum lateral deflection of the rear axle under LC2 cornering must not exceed 2.5 mm to preserve handling geometry; (R4) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC1 (compressive chain-pull path) must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 3.0; (R5) first torsional natural frequency (swingarm alone, with axle fixed, pivot free) must exceed 100 Hz to avoid chatter coupling with the 100 Hz rear-wheel-hop mode typical of 200 mm stroke suspension; (R6) swingarm mass must not exceed 3.2 kg per the benchmark chassis-mass target tables. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 7020-T6 aluminum swingarm for a collegiate petrol-category prototype racing motorcycle (Moto3-adjacent geometry) with a 250 cc single producing 30 kW / 27 N-m at the countershaft sprocket. The swingarm is machined from a single forged billet (no welds) with integral pivot lugs, rear axle forks with adjuster slots, a lateral bridge, and a left-arm brake-caliper mounting ear. Apply FIM racing-motorcycle chassis practice with yield/2.0 safety factor on primary structure.

Envelope: pivot-to-axle 560 mm, overall ~580 x 250 x 100 mm, twin-sided box-section arms.

Load cases.
- LC1 longitudinal drive + bump: 4.0 kN forward chain-pull at the rear axle combined with 2.7 kN vertical bump (3.5 g on rear-biased 78 kg), pivot bore fixed.
- LC2 cornering + braking: 3.5 kN lateral at the rear axle plus 1.8 kN rearward braking.
- LC3 wheelie landing: 4.9 kN vertical down at the rear axle (5 g).
Also check a 10^7-cycle fatigue spectrum off LC1 at notch features and a modal case (axle fixed, pivot free) for torsional chatter coupling.

prompt.title: Electric motorcycle swingarm benchmark (petrol-category 250 cc spec engine)
prompt.brief: Monolithic 7020-T6 billet swingarm; pivot-to-axle 560 mm; twin-sided box arms; Moto3 envelope; FIM chassis safety factors.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Exhaust-heat standoff is outside swingarm path.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero loads on swingarm negligible at Moto3 speeds.

eval_coverage: fatigue at pivot bore + axle slot notches
